# Modonome

> Governance and guardrails for AI coding agents. Modonome is an off-by-default
> engineering loop that keeps making, checking, and merging in separate hands,
> runs every change through gates that live in your CI, and turns on only when a
> repository owner arms it. It is a prompt and a set of scripts that run inside a
> harness you already have: a coding agent, a CI job, or a human session.

## What it does

- Keeps the maker, the checker, and the merge authority as separate roles, so no agent approves its own work.
- Runs an anti-gaming ratchet in CI, outside the agent's write scope, that holds a merge when tests, types, or coverage are weakened. Language-aware across JavaScript, Python, Java, and .NET.
- Adopts your existing CI, code owners, and branch rules. There is no new process to learn.
- Promotes real corrections into durable rules, so the loop improves over time.
- Provides an operator control panel, a Merkle-verified repository snapshot for trustworthy agent context, and compliance evidence and attestation through an MCP server.

## AgentProof

AgentProof is the adversarial benchmark for agent gate-integrity. It runs a real attack for each scenario and confirms the control catches it. Modonome scores 25/25 HARDENED on the normative set, and 10/10 on the extended set, for 35/35 total. Conformance: HARDENED is 25/25 normative, PARTIAL is 20 to 24, UNHARDENED is 19 or fewer. Run it with `node agentproof/runner.mjs`.

## Get started

- Preview safely, writing nothing: `npx modonome dry-run .`
- Scaffold local state, autonomy still at rest: `npx modonome scaffold . --write`
- Prove the controls on your own setup: `node agentproof/runner.mjs`

- AgentProof: https://modonome.com/agentproof/
